children.text: We open with a shot of the Beijing Olympics 2022 emblem. The featured woman of this video flies through the logo, and we see various Chinese landmarks and a snowy landscape in the background. The video imitates an animated style, but the generated elements of the setting have several errors. We see crooked windows on the Great Wall, ski lifts with lost details, and colors that abruptly disappear. The woman then flies through the emblem of the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics. As she moves through the logo, her clothes transform into a generic East Asian dress. This is an odd detail — we can assume that the change in clothing is intended to correspond to the location of the Winter Olympics (in this case, Korea). Still, this dress hardly resembles traditional Korean clothing. This reveals generative AI’s inability to check itself unless prompted to do so. One can assume that had this video been created with more human involvement, this error would not have appeared in the final product.

We quickly move to a shot of the Sochi Olympics 2014 logo, with a wide shot of snowy mountains and a lake, intended to imitate the Russian landscape; the video no longer has a cartoon-ish style, this shot is photorealistic
Vancouver Olympics 2010 logo appears as a park sign, landscape presumably Canadian) is shown; style of animation is infogrpahic-like; focus is on the nature of Vancouver/Canada in general
For some reason the main woman now has multi-colored hair all of a sudden; she snowboards for a couple seconds, and goes off screen with the Torino Olympics 2006 logo appearing

Snowy mountain of the Torino Olympics morphis into a flag with a wide shot of a mountain range, the Salt Lake City Olympics 2002 logo is on the flag; transition from cartoonish style to photorealistic, once again.
A generic perching bird flies around the flag, the shot follows the bird in flight, and it perches onto a pair of ski poles; the Nagano Olympics 1998 logo appears
Bird begins to take off, and transforms into the main woman in a burst of warm colors; Woman sleds through colorful tunnel
the sledding woman fades; Lillehammer Olympics 1994 logo appears on screen, which is entirely pink/orange
the main woman appears once again, as a figure skater this time. Posters lay around her, one of them being the Lake Placid 1990 Olympics
The Innsbruck Olympics 1976 logo appears
The main woman skiis on the white strip on the Innsbruck logo
As the woman skiis, we get an extreme close-up of her eye
We move through the iris of her eye to get the Sapporo Olympics 1972 logo

The Sapporo Olympics logo quickly morphs into the Grenoble Olympics 1968 logo; We get a rapid fire sequence of morphing logos, with Sapporo 1972 Olympics morphing into the Grenoble 1964 Olympics, to the Squaw Valley 1960 Olympics, and finally into the Cortina d'Ampezzo 1956 Olympics.
The Oslo Olympics 1952 logo and scenery of mountains and Olympic/Norwegian flags appear
Two figures ski past the flags
The sky darkens into a starry sky with fireworks; the skiing figures move past the St. Moritz Olympics 1948 graphic
The main woman skiis past posters featuring other past Olympic games; she skiis, gets hit by a snowball and falls
A poster featuring the St. Moritz Olympics 1928 unfolds as a transition
Woman playing hockey, shoots hockey puck, which flies across the setting into a surreal depiction of a phoenix flying over snowy mountains and several Olympic athletes
Chamonix Olympics 1924 frames the phoenix bird
Cut to the main woman standing on phoenix-like bird, for some reason
Woman falls off bird, lands in snow, video ends
